sn3.6:1.2: As he was sitting to one side, King Pasenadi Kosala said to the Blessed One:
sn3.6:1.3: “Just now, lord, while I was alone in seclusion, this train of thought arose in my awareness:
sn3.6:1.4: ‘Few are those people in the world who, when acquiring lavish wealth, don’t become intoxicated & heedless, don’t become greedy for sensuality, and don’t mistreat other beings.
sn3.6:1.5: Many more are those who, when acquiring lavish wealth, become intoxicated & heedless, become greedy for sensuality, and mistreat other beings.’”
sn3.6:2.1: “That’s the way it is, great king! That’s the way it is!
sn3.6:2.2: Few are those people in the world who, when acquiring lavish wealth, don’t become intoxicated & heedless, don’t become greedy for sensuality, and don’t mistreat other beings.
sn3.6:2.3: Many more are those who, when acquiring lavish wealth, become intoxicated & heedless, become greedy for sensuality, and mistreat other beings.”
sn3.6:2.4: That is what the Blessed One said. Having said that, the One Well-Gone, the Teacher, said further:
sn3.6:3.3: they don’t awaken to the fact that they’ve gone too far—
sn3.6:3.4: like deer into a trap laid out.
